adds.rationale: Rocket Lab is the clearest US-listed pure-play on the profile's #1 priority next wave: commercial small-sat launch entered its revenue-generating phase in 2021, a second launch pad (LC-1B) opened with a successful February 28 mission, and a multi-mission Synspective contract demonstrates repeatable commercial cadence.

news_evidence.summary: Rocket Lab executes 'The Owl's Night Continues' Electron mission from the new Launch Complex 1B at Mahia, delivering Synspective's StriX-β SAR satellite on the pad's first flight.
Mission is first of three dedicated Electron launches contracted for Synspective's SAR constellation in 2022-2023, a booked commercial backlog across multiple customers.
Orbital Today reports the successful inaugural LC-1B flight; RKLB now operates two active pads and is building a US launch site in Virginia.

adds.rationale: The Global X Robotics and AI ETF captures the profile's #2 priority next wave at the moment the structural demand driver — a persistent multi-year manufacturing labor shortage — is accelerating capital expenditure on industrial automation across US and European manufacturers.

news_evidence.summary: RoboticsTomorrow notes a projected 6-million-worker manufacturing shortage by 2030 and accelerating deployment of collaborative robots.
ABB Q4 2021 results: orders across Robotics and Discrete Automation grew 18% YoY with broad-based strength across regions and segments.

adds.rationale: Russia's invasion of Ukraine shattered the assumption that Russian-sourced uranium enrichment (42% of global capacity) is reliable; utilities are bidding spot uranium to a 10-year high to lock in Western-origin supply — the early commercial inflection the profile identifies as the entry signal for the nuclear fission wave.

news_evidence.summary: S&P Global: U3O8 spot hits $50.75/lb — highest since June 2012 — as utilities replace Russian-origin supply; Sprott made its largest single-day purchase Feb 25.
Bloomberg: 'Uranium Surges to Highest Since Fukushima on Russian War' — Ux U3O8 reaches $59.75/lb; US weighing sanctions on Rosatom (42% of global enrichment).
Bloomberg: investors flood into uranium following the invasion, citing Rosatom's JVs with Kazatomprom (45% of global primary uranium) as a dual supply chokepoint.

rationale_overall: The AI bucket (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA) is already at the four-ticker cap, so all three adds open exposure to the profile's priority next-wave categories. RKLB fills rockets/spacecraft on its Feb 28 LC-1B commercial pad expansion. BOTZ fills robotics as the labor-shortage driver pulls forward automation capex (ABB record Q4 2021 orders). URA fills nuclear on the Ukraine supply shock driving uranium spot to a 10-year high. The watchlist reaches its hard cap of 8 with zero removes. Defense/rearmament (Germany's Zeitenwende) is noted but deferred since US primes already partially re-rated within weeks.
